reasoning: South Sudan signed the AfCFTA and the treaty has been pending before its National Assembly since 2021. However, as of January 2025, South Sudan remained one of the seven African nations yet to ratify the agreement. Recent political developments show that South Sudan's parliament recently approved an extension of the transitional government by 24 months, indicating severe political gridlock, overriding domestic priorities, and dysfunction. There is no trace of recent news from late 2024 or early 2025 to suggest that ratifying the AfCFTA is currently on the active legislative agenda. Given the massive delays so far, the extremely short time horizon (less than two months between the May 2025 knowledge cutoff and the July 2025 resolution date), and the ongoing political crisis within the country, it is highly unlikely that South Sudan will finalize ratification before July 1, 2025.
